zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir William Hawes於從1347年1月9日到1348年1月8日出生在英國素福爾可瓦爾申馬 勒 維洛福斯時，他的父親John Hawys39歲，母親Hillary de Cranmere28歲。他和Mrs. Amya Hawys至少有2個兒子。他去世於從1408年1月10日到1409年1月9日在英國英格蘭素福爾可瓦爾申馬 勒 維洛福斯。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir William Hawes于从1347年1月9日到1348年1月8日出生在英国素福爾可瓦爾申馬 勒 維洛福斯时，他的父亲John Hawys39岁，母亲Hillary de Cranmere28岁。他和Mrs. Amya Hawys至少有2个儿子。他去世于从1408年1月10日到1409年1月9日在英国英格兰素福爾可瓦爾申馬 勒 維洛福斯。

nameMeanings.name: Hawes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: of Norman origin, from Anglo-Norman French and Middle English Haueis, Hawis, from an Old French form of the ancient Germanic female personal name Hadewidis (from had- ‘war’ + wid- with a range of possible original meanings).
variant of Haw , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Haw
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Haw(e) which, like Daw(e), was probably a rhyming pet form of Middle English Raw (Ralph). Similar rhyming pet forms of other Anglo-Norman male personal names beginning with R- are found in Hick and Dick for Rick (Richard) and Hob and Dob for Rob(ert). See also Hawkin .
English: topographic name for someone who lived by an enclosure, from Middle English hawe ‘enclosure’ (Old English haga or Old Norse hagi). Compare Haugh .
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surnames 何 and 賀, see   He   1 and 2.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir William Hawes was born from 9 January 1347 to 8 January 1348, in Walsham le Willows, Suffolk, England, his father, John Hawys, was 39 and his mother, Hillary de Cranmere, was 28. He had at least 2 sons with Mrs. Amya Hawys. He died from 10 January 1408 to 9 January 1409, in Walsham le Willows, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom.

Januar 1348 in Walsham le Willows, Suffolk, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater John Hawys 39 und seine Mutter Hillary de Cranmere 28. Er hatte mit Mrs. Amya Hawys mindestens 2 Söhne. ko.lifeSummary: Sir William Hawes(이)가 1347년 1월 9일부터 1348년 1월 8일까지 영국 수폴크 월샤 일 윌로스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Hawys(은)는 39세였고, 그의 어머니 Hillary de Cranmere(은)는 28세였다. 그는 Mrs. Amya Hawys(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1408년 1월 10일부터 1409년 1월 9일까지 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 월샤 일 윌로스에서 사망했다.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir William Hawes nació desde 9 de enero de 1347 hasta 8 de enero de 1348, en Walsham le Willows, Suffolk, Inglaterra, su papá, John Hawys, tenía 39 años y su mamá, Hillary de Cranmere, tenía 28 años. Tuvo por lo menos 2 hijos con Mrs. Amya Hawys. Murió desde 10 de enero de 1408 hasta 9 de enero de 1409, en Walsham le Willows, Suffolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
